Equal parts dive watch and field watch, with the convenience of a quartz movement, this is a truly versatile time piece. The matte finished stainless steel case is slim yet substantial, feels great, and hides wear very well. The green dial is a great pop of color, and plays with light very well- sometimes it's as fresh as an evergreen, and sometimes it could be mistaken for black. If I'm being honest, the black rubber strap has never been for me (I don't dive with it...), but I'll include a nice brown leather strap that pairs nicely with the dial as well. Date window and uni-directional dive bezel round out the features. Will ship with a non-original, Breitling branded soft case.

A fantastic grab and go watch that I'm only getting rid of it because I've ended up with the exact same watch with a white dial on bracelet!

This is a classic Breitling Aerospace (Reference F65062), the quintessential "pilot's tool watch" known for its incredible lightweight durability and functional design. This specific model features the sought-after titanium and 18k yellow gold "two-tone" configuration with a vibrant blue dial.

Key Technical Specifications:
Movement: Breitling Caliber 65 (Quartz) with multifunction digital/analog display.
Case: 40mm high-grade titanium, only 9mm thick for maximum comfort.
Bezel: Unidirectional rotating titanium bezel with four signature 18k yellow gold rider tabs.
Functions: Features a 1/100th second chronograph, countdown timer, second time zone (GMT), alarm, and perpetual calendar.
Crystal: Scratch-resistant sapphire crystal.
Bracelet: Original titanium and gold-tone bracelet with a secure folding clasp.
Inclusions: Includes original Breitling presentation box. Please note that original papers/manuals are not included. The watch's authenticity is guaranteed by the correct reference (F65062) and serial number

Condition Note: The watch is in excellent pre-owned condition and functions perfectly.

For sale is my Breitling Aviator 8 Automatic Day & Date 41mm, reference A45330. This sophisticated pilot's watch draws on Breitling's deep aviation heritage while offering refined, versatile design ideal for daily wear. The 41mm stainless steel case strikes an excellent balance between presence and comfort, measuring about 11.4mm thick with a comfortable 48mm lug-to-lug distance. It features a bidirectional rotating bezel for timing tasks, a screw-down crown, and solid caseback, providing 100m water resistance suitable for everyday adventures.

The dial presents a clean aviation-inspired layout with luminous hands and markers that ensure strong legibility in any lighting. Prominent day and date windows sit at 12 and 6 o'clock, adding practical functionality without overwhelming the balanced composition. Powered by the Breitling Caliber 45, an automatic movement based on a reliable architecture and certified as a COSC chronometer, it offers approximately 40 hours of power reserve and smooth 28,800 vph performance.

The crystal is scratch-resistant sapphire with anti-reflective coating for clear viewing from any angle. The watch wears comfortably and allows easy strap changes to suit different occasions.

Overall, this reference stands out for its understated elegance compared to Breitling's more complex chronographs, while retaining the brand's signature pilot aesthetic. It makes an excellent choice for business settings, casual outings, or weekend travel.

This Breitling Chronomat AB0110 features a bold 44mm stainless steel case paired with a striking black dial with applied Roman numerals, giving it a distinctive and upscale presence. The watch is powered by Breitling’s in-house Caliber 01 automatic chronograph movement, COSC-certified for accuracy and offering an impressive 70-hour power reserve.

The dial includes three chronograph sub-dials, luminous hands and markers, and a date window at 4:30. A tachymeter scale lines the inner bezel, while the iconic Chronomat rotating bezel with rider tabs completes the signature Breitling look. Finished on a solid stainless steel bracelet, this watch delivers a perfect balance of sport, luxury, and everyday wearability.

A standout Chronomat configuration that’s becoming harder to find—ideal for collectors or anyone looking for a powerful, unmistakable wrist presence.

For sale is one of my absolute favorites in the Breitling catalog, the Superocean Heritage 42, reference AB2010, a modern dive watch that blends classic styling with a strong, contemporary dial.

The standout feature here is the green sunray dial. In softer light it appears deep and almost understated, but when the light hits it directly the dial comes alive with a vibrant sunburst effect. The tone shifts throughout the day, giving the watch a lot more personality than a typical monochrome diver.

The design of the Superocean Heritage line pulls directly from Breitling’s original 1957 Superocean, with the distinctive arrow hour hand, minimalist markers, and clean bezel layout. It has a vintage influence, but the finishing and proportions keep it feeling firmly modern.

The 42mm stainless steel case wears comfortably on the wrist and strikes a good balance between presence and everyday wearability. The polished ceramic bezel adds depth and durability while maintaining the sleek profile that defines the Heritage series.

Inside is the Breitling Caliber B20, an automatic movement based on the Tudor MT5612 architecture. It’s COSC-certified, offers a 70-hour power reserve, and is known for its durability and accuracy.
The result is a diver that feels refined enough for daily wear while still carrying the DNA of a true tool watch.

Condition: This example will come in very good, used condition. There is minor wear on the case in the form of handling marks. There are some hairline scratches on the caseback. The watch is keeping great time according to the timegraph and does not require a service at this time. The factory warranty is valid until 12/2031, so wear this piece worry free for years.

The crystal is very clear. I honestly cannot see any scratches on the crystal. I have been careful not to use any cleaning chemicals so that the anti-reflective coating stayed in good condition. There are a few minor scratches throughout the rest of the watch, but the watch is so highly polished that it is difficult to notice them. The only exception to this is the clasp where your wrist would touch a table. Very light scratches on the mirror finished clasp are definitely visible there. There seems to be a missing piece on the crown. It's barely noticeable so I didn't notice it until I was taking these photos to list this watch for sale. I think there was a Breitling logo on the crown when I first bought it, but there isn't one there now. I don't know how long it has been missing. The photos clearly show that part of the watch, so you can decide for yourself how much it matters. Bracelet included. My wrist is 7 inches. I don't have any extra links.

A stunning example of a Breitling Transocean Chronograph. Full set, in excellent condition.

From the golden age of air travel to your wrist, the Breitling Transocean was born in 1958 as a watch designed for the sophisticated traveler who needed a timepiece as durable as it was elegant.

This modern Transocean Chronograph carries that legacy forward, pairing a rich brown dial with rose gold accents and a brilliant diamond bezel. It’s a true blend of vintage inspiration and modern luxury.

But this watch is more than just a pretty face. The clean, elegant design, coupled with its chronograph functionality, pays a direct homage to Breitling's deep aviation heritage. It’s a timepiece that looks just as great in a cocktail lounge as it does in a cockpit.

The Breitling Top Time B01 Ford Thunderbird (Reference AB01766A1A1X1) is a 41mm stainless steel chronograph that pays tribute to the first-generation 1954 Ford Thunderbird. Its design blends a "clean" 1950s aesthetic with sporty racing elements.

Case and Dimensions
Material: Brushed and polished stainless steel.
Diameter: 41 mm (down from the 42mm size of earlier Top Time models).
Thickness: Approximately 13.3 mm.
Lug-to-Lug: 50.36 mm.
Crystal: A cambered sapphire crystal, glare-proofed on both sides, with a highly domed shape meant to evoke vintage Plexiglass.
Case Back: A transparent sapphire exhibition case back that displays the Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01 movement and features the Thunderbird logo.

Dial and Hands
Main Dial: A matte white dial described as "quiet and reserved" compared to other models in the collection.
Sub-dials: Three contrasting "squircle" (rounded-square) sub-dials designed to look like vintage dashboard gauges.
Branding: The Ford Thunderbird logo is prominently featured in blue/teal at the 12 o'clock position (or within the sub-dials depending on the specific edition).
Accents: A white outer rim features a tachymeter scale with orange and yellow highlights.
Luminescence: Super-LumiNova coating on the hands and baton hour markers for visibility in low light.

Strap and Hardware
Material: Red calfskin leather with a "racing" or "rally" style, featuring circular perforations.
Clasp: A stainless steel folding deployant clasp with a twin trigger release.
Pushers: Vintage-inspired pump-style chronograph pushers

STRIKING Breitling Chronomat 44 with Tungsten Gray Dial on Stainless Steel Pilot Bracelet! Comes with the original Breitling boxes and was SERVICED at my watchmaker in November 2025, a huge value-add. The dial is commonly referred to as black, but I think it’s a mix between black & gray, being slightly darker than gray, but certainly not as light as the blackeye gray dial. Sometimes, they are even referred to as dark grey dials or “carbon black” dials, flip a coin on whether it’s black or dark gray. I think “Tungsten Gray” is the closest. These are very hard to find, not common at all. I like them because of the duality in the dials and how unique they are!

The watch is clean overall with minor wear, nothing notable. Keeping good time when tested on my timegrapher! Do excuse dust/lint in the pictures from the photography process.\ Ready to be on wrist.

The bracelet can accommodate a 7.4 inch wrist at maximum without additional link.

Breitling Emergency E5632110/B279
Full Collector Set | Factory Overhaul 3-17-2025 | Complete Kit | Titanium
The Brand: Breitling Heritage & Instrument Legacy
Founded in 1884, Breitling has built its reputation as one of Switzerland’s most respected aviation tool-watch manufacturers. The brand became synonymous with cockpit instruments, precision chronographs, and professional pilot equipment throughout the 20th century.

Breitling was the official supplier to numerous aviation units and is one of the few brands that built its identity around functional instruments rather than jewelry. The Emergency line represents the purest expression of that philosophy — a watch designed not just to tell time, but to potentially save a life.

The Model: The Breitling Emergency – A Watch Unlike Any Other
The Breitling Emergency was the world’s first wristwatch with an integrated personal locator beacon (PLB). Designed for pilots, adventurers, and professionals operating in remote environments, it features a built-in micro-transmitter antenna system engineered to broadcast an emergency signal on the international aviation distress frequency.

This is not a gimmick complication — it is a purpose-built survival instrument.

Because of the transmitter system, proper servicing is critical. Many Emergency models on the market are out of service, deactivated, or incomplete. This example has been overhaul serviced by Breitling on 3-17-2025, offering mechanical integrity and proper system inspection — something serious buyers should demand.

For collectors, the Emergency occupies a unique space:

One of the boldest functional watches ever produced

A conversation piece even among seasoned collectors

A true professional instrument

Increasingly desirable in complete, documented full-set condition

What Makes This Example Exceptional
This is a true full collector set, including:

For sale is a stunning Breitling Superocean Heritage II in the highly sought-after blue dial configuration. This is a complete, full-set example in excellent condition, representing a rare opportunity to acquire one of Breitling's most iconic sport watches with remaining manufacturer's warranty.
Condition
The watch presents in excellent condition throughout. The case retains its sharp lines with minimal signs of wear, and the sapphire crystal is completely clear with no scratches or marks. The blue ceramic unidirectional bezel is in excellent shape with no chips, fading, or damage. The dial and hands are flawless. The only cosmetic imperfection is a few minor, light scratches on the deployment clasp — consistent with normal everyday wear and not visible during wear. Overall, this watch presents far above average for its age.
The Watch
The Superocean Heritage II is Breitling's love letter to their iconic 1957 dive watch, blending vintage-inspired design with modern Swiss engineering. The sunburst blue dial is one of the most versatile and wearable colorways in the collection — equally at home with a suit as it is with a t-shirt. The blue ceramic bezel adds a cohesive, polished look that has made this reference a favorite among collectors and enthusiasts alike.
Powering the watch is Breitling's Calibre B20 — a robust automatic movement derived from the acclaimed Tudor MT5612 platform, offering an impressive 70-hour power reserve and COSC chronometer certification, ensuring accuracy to within -4/+6 seconds per day.

Made to move between the waves and the waterfront. The Superocean Heritage is no ordinary sea watch—it never was. When Breitling first launched it in 1957, it took a different tack from other dive watches of the era. The Ref. 1004 (a sleek time-only piece) and Ref. 807 (the world’s first dedicated dive chronograph) weren’t just about exploring the underwater world—they were about looking good doing it. The spear-and-arrow hands, ceramic rotating bezel, and naturally sleek proportions made it a favorite of stylish adventurers who lived for the sea and the scene. Today, that philosophy is still at the core of the Superocean Heritage. It’s got the soul of the 1950s, but designed for today. The latest Superocean Heritage builds on everything that made the original distinctive—and sharpens it. The chronograph is powered by the Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01, Breitling’s industry-leading timekeeping movement. A ceramic-inlayed bezel adds exceptional scratch resistance, while integrated mesh-metal or mesh-rubber bracelets fit snugly against the case, secured with folding clasps for a clean, watertight finish. Tone-on-tone subdials, a discreet date window at six o’clock, and the original signature hands—arrow for hours, spear for minutes—ensure legibility and balance, above or below the surface.

This watch comes as a Full Set (Box & Papers).

Breitling Superocean Automatic 42 | Deep Blue Dial Ref. A17375E71C1A1 | 42mm | Stainless Steel | Full Set

Price: $4,150

The Breitling Superocean has long been a pillar of the diving community, and this latest iteration, Ref. A17375E71C1A1, is a brilliant tribute to that professional heritage. My own love for the deep blue and the diving hobby has always drawn me to watches that feel at home in the water, and this piece captures that soul perfectly. It feels like a genuine nod to diving professionals everywhere who require legibility and reliability above all else.
What I find most captivating about this design is the return of the signature square minute hand—a bold, geometric throwback to the 1960s "Slow Motion" Superocean. The high-contrast dial and ceramic inlay bezel make it incredibly striking in person. At a versatile 42mm, I’ve found that this case size is the "goldilocks" of divers; it has enough wrist presence for a wetsuit but is slim and refined enough to accommodate a dress shirt for a formal dinner. It is a true go-anywhere, do-anything tool watch with a high-fashion edge.

* Original Stainless Steel Mesh-inspired Bracelet.

For sale is my Breitling Aviator 8 Automatic Day & Date 41mm, reference A45330. This sophisticated pilot's watch draws on Breitling's deep aviation heritage while offering refined, versatile design ideal for daily wear. The 41mm stainless steel case strikes an excellent balance between presence and comfort, measuring about 11.4mm thick with a comfortable 48mm lug-to-lug distance. It features a bidirectional rotating bezel for timing tasks, a screw-down crown, and solid caseback, providing 100m water resistance suitable for everyday adventures.

The dial presents a clean aviation-inspired layout with luminous hands and markers that ensure strong legibility in any lighting. Prominent day and date windows sit at 12 and 6 o'clock, adding practical functionality without overwhelming the balanced composition. Powered by the Breitling Caliber 45, an automatic movement based on a reliable architecture and certified as a COSC chronometer, it offers approximately 40 hours of power reserve and smooth 28,800 vph performance.

The crystal is scratch-resistant sapphire with anti-reflective coating for clear viewing from any angle. The watch wears comfortably and allows easy strap changes to suit different occasions.

Overall, this reference stands out for its understated elegance compared to Breitling's more complex chronographs, while retaining the brand's signature pilot aesthetic. It makes an excellent choice for business settings, casual outings, or weekend travel.

Breitling Emergency E5632110/B279
Full Collector Set | Factory Overhaul 3-17-2025 | Complete Kit | Titanium
The Brand: Breitling Heritage & Instrument Legacy
Founded in 1884, Breitling has built its reputation as one of Switzerland’s most respected aviation tool-watch manufacturers. The brand became synonymous with cockpit instruments, precision chronographs, and professional pilot equipment throughout the 20th century.

Breitling was the official supplier to numerous aviation units and is one of the few brands that built its identity around functional instruments rather than jewelry. The Emergency line represents the purest expression of that philosophy — a watch designed not just to tell time, but to potentially save a life.

The Model: The Breitling Emergency – A Watch Unlike Any Other
The Breitling Emergency was the world’s first wristwatch with an integrated personal locator beacon (PLB). Designed for pilots, adventurers, and professionals operating in remote environments, it features a built-in micro-transmitter antenna system engineered to broadcast an emergency signal on the international aviation distress frequency.

This is not a gimmick complication — it is a purpose-built survival instrument.

Because of the transmitter system, proper servicing is critical. Many Emergency models on the market are out of service, deactivated, or incomplete. This example has been overhaul serviced by Breitling on 3-17-2025, offering mechanical integrity and proper system inspection — something serious buyers should demand.

For collectors, the Emergency occupies a unique space:

One of the boldest functional watches ever produced

A conversation piece even among seasoned collectors

A true professional instrument

Increasingly desirable in complete, documented full-set condition

What Makes This Example Exceptional
This is a true full collector set, including:

Original Breitling Display Case

Original VHS instructional video

Manual

Warranty booklet

Chronometer certificate

Authorized distributor locations booklet

Breitling plastic sales medallion

Original titanium bracelet

Extra links

Breitling screwdriver

Breitling frequency tester

Custom leather strap

Breitling Emergency Watch

Complete Breitling overhaul service paperwork (3-17-2025) and service card

Full sets with the VHS, medallion, tester, and factory service documentation are becoming increasingly difficult to source together.

Condition
Excellent serviced condition. The watch has been freshly overhauled by Breitling and gently used while presenting a well-maintained, collector-grade example.

Important Buyer Information (Please Read Carefully)
• This is a professional instrument watch with a factory transmitter system.
• The emergency transmitter is for genuine emergency use only. Improper activation may result in significant governmental fines and rescue response liability.
• The watch has been factory serviced as of 3-17-2025; however, buyer assumes responsibility for understanding operational requirements and regulatory considerations of ownership.


If you are serious about the safety capability this watch offers, buying one that is out of service defeats the purpose. This example provides mechanical peace of mind and collector completeness.

The 'Jupiter' was introduced in 1980 by Breitling in the year following the take-over of the company by Mr Ernest Schneider. Along with the closely related 'Mars' and 'Pluton' chronographs, the Jupiter was designed for military pilots but also with an eye on the civilian aviation market. There were various bezel options and differences between the three models but the movements and therefore the functions are identical.

At this time, all Breitling pilot's watches were considered part of the 'Navitimer' range and the Jupiter was named 'NAVITIMER QUARTZ 2300' on the dial. It has a non-ratcheted combination bezel comprising a circular slide-rule (Type 52 derived from the Navitimer ref 806) and a wind-rose (compass degrees) scale. Between the these two scales are flight levels in two colours for VFR/IFR and East/West flight separation.

Breitling’s iconic pilot’s chronograph – for the journey. In 1952, Willy Breitling developed a wrist-worn chronograph with a circular slide rule that would allow pilots to perform all necessary flight calculations. Two years later, the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) announced the design as its official timepiece. The “navigation timer” – or Navitimer – was born. The AOPA was (and remains) the largest pilots’ club in the world, counting nearly every US aviator among its ranks. As the defacto pilot’s watch throughout the glory days of civil aviation, the Navitimer was worn by airline captains and aircraft enthusiasts. It even made its way into space on the wrist of astronaut Scott Carpenter in 1962. And it wasn’t only pilots drawn to the watch’s irrepressible aesthetic. Celebrities of the day such as Miles Davis and Serge Gainsbourg were devotees, proving that the Navitimer had style as well as function. There have been many iterations of Breitling’s icon since its debut 70 years ago, but this new Navitimer captures its most classic features, while enhancing them with modern refinements. A flattened slide rule and a domed crystal create the illusion of a sleeker profile. Alternating polished and brushed metal elements give a lustrous, yet understated finish. Most notably, new colors in shades of blue, green and copper, define the updated dial options. And if there is one update sure to spark nostalgia, it’s the return of the AOPA wings to their original position at 12 o’clock. For 70 years, Breitling’s original pilot’s watch has been beloved by aviators and tastemakers in equal measure. Worn by astronauts in space and the biggest stars on earth, it is Breitling’s most iconic timepiece and one of the most recognizable watches of all time.
41mm Brushed & polished stainless steel case.
Stainless steel bi-directional rotating bezel with slide-rule. The circular slide-rule allows pilots to perform necessary flight calculations.
Silver dial with Copper Color Hands.
Brown Alligator Strap with Fold Clasp.
Features the AOPA wings logo at 12 o'clock.
Copper hour & minute hands with luminous fill.
Copper index hour markers with luminous tips.
Fine printed minutes/seconds & 1/4 seconds fractions track.
Small seconds sub-dial at 9 o'clock.
Date window at 6 o'clock.
Chronograph/Stopwatch functions:
Column wheel mechanism.
Large Red central seconds hand counter.
30 minute counter sub-dial at 3 o'clock.
12 hour counter sub-dial at 6 o'clock.
Breitling in house caliber B01 beats at 28,800 vph, contains 47 jewels & has an approximate power reserve of 70 hours.

The Breitling Superocean has long been a pillar of the diving community, and this latest iteration, Ref. A17375E71C1A1, is a brilliant tribute to that professional heritage. My own love for the deep blue and the diving hobby has always drawn me to watches that feel at home in the water, and this piece captures that soul perfectly. It feels like a genuine nod to diving professionals everywhere who require legibility and reliability above all else.
What I find most captivating about this design is the return of the signature square minute hand—a bold, geometric throwback to the 1960s "Slow Motion" Superocean. The high-contrast dial and ceramic inlay bezel make it incredibly striking in person. At a versatile 42mm, I’ve found that this case size is the "goldilocks" of divers; it has enough wrist presence for a wetsuit but is slim and refined enough to accommodate a dress shirt for a formal dinner. It is a true go-anywhere, do-anything tool watch with a high-fashion edge.

The Breitling Old Navitimer Patrouille de France Ref. Presented in excellent condition, with a clean dial, sharp case, and fully functional chronograph, this watch represents a rare opportunity to own a distinctive and historically meaningful Breitling pilot’s chronograph.

Breitling’s supercharged watch for your every pursuit. The Super Chronomat is inspired by the original 1980s Chronomat that Breitling created for the hotshots of aerobatic squadrons. The boldest of all Chronomat watches, the Super Chronomat features several ceramic inserts – on the bezel, on the chronograph pushers and on the crown – as well as a Rouleaux-inspired rubber strap. Still, this all-purpose sports watch won’t get in the way of your sense of style. The Super Chronomat comes in several colour and metal variations, from stainless steel to 18 k red gold. It either features the iconic Rouleaux bracelet or a rubber Rouleaux-inspired strap, matched with a folding clasp. The Super Chronomat B01 44 is powered by the Breitling Manufacture Calibre 01, a COSC-certified chronometer.

44mm Brushed & polished 18kt rose gold and stainless steel case.

In the early 1940s, Willy Breitling began designing a chronograph that would be functional but also undeniably elegant and infinitely wearable—in his words, “the unmistakable stamp of impeccable taste.” He called this new line the Premier. To create the modern Premier, Breitling preserved distinctive vintage details, including the smooth fixed bezel, twin-register dial, streamlined rectangular pushers, and applied Arabic numerals. Powering the timepiece is a self-winding movement—the COSC-certified Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01—designed for maximum precision, reliability, and functionality. The condition of this timepiece is very good, with only very minor superficial scratching throughout the case and bracelet from minimal wear. This watch is accompanied by a full set, with all accessories pictured.

Breitling’s most versatile Navitimer—for the journey. The Navitimer is one of the most recognizable watches ever made. It’s on collectors’ lists of the greatest watches of all time. What began in 1952 as a tool for pilots has gone on to mean something profound to every single person who has had this timepiece along on their personal journey. The Navitimer Automatic and Automatic GMT 41 distill the iconic look, omitting the chronograph yet preserving a balanced dial composition. The central placement of the 24-hour scale on the GMT and the Automatic’s clean face, ensure the intricate slide rule stands out while streamlining the overall aesthetics. Further refining the collection, the notched bezel was incorporated for a contemporary touch, and alternating polished and brushed finishes add a dynamic play of light. Straps in alligator leather and bracelets with a seamless butterfly clasp offer comfort and convenience. The palette includes colorful dials, encased in a choice of stainless steel or 18 k red gold. This reinvention of the Navitimer, marrying the classic with the contemporary, is tailored for enthusiasts and the style-conscious alike, promising Breitling’s commitment to heritage and innovation in each timepiece.

In the early 1940s, Willy Breitling was already dreaming of a time after the war, when utility would give way to unbridled optimism. He began designing a chronograph that would be functional but also undeniably elegant and infinitely wearable—in his words, “the unmistakable stamp of impeccable taste.” He called this new line the Premier. To create its modern Premier, Breitling preserved distinctive vintage-Premier details, including the smooth fixed bezel, twin-register dial, streamlined rectangular pushers and applied Arabic numerals. The Premier B01 Chronograph 42 is available in an array of dial colors and case materials. It comes on a choice of classic alligator leather strap or sleek 7-row metal bracelet. Today’s Premier also lives up to Willy Breitling’s uncompromising technical standards with its exceptional self-winding movement—the COSC-certified Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01—designed for maximum precision, reliability and functionality.

The Breitling Avenger II Seawolf Blacksteel Limited Edition differs from other Seawolf models in several ways, most notably its unique black steel case, vibrant yellow "Cobra" dial, and status as a collectible limited-edition timepiece.
Aesthetic and design differences
Case material: The most significant difference is the use of a black steel case, which is standard stainless steel treated with a durable carbon-based (DLC) black finish. This gives the watch a sleek, matte, "stealth fighter"-inspired look that contrasts with the traditional silver-toned stainless steel used on most other Seawolf models.
Color scheme: The specific model you identified is known as the "Code Yellow" or "Cobra Yellow" edition due to its bright, eye-catching yellow dial. The black case and bezel provide a high-contrast background for the yellow dial and luminous markers, improving legibility. Most standard Seawolfs have a black, blue, or gray dial.
Dial details: The black steel model also features distinct dial elements, such as a seconds hand with a yellow tip, which stands out against the dark case. Some standard Seawolf models, depending on the generation, have different colored hands and markers.
Rarity and collectibility
Limited edition: The M17331E2 model was produced as a limited edition of 1,000 pieces. This makes it a rare and collectible watch, often more sought-after than the standard, serially produced Seawolf watches.
Discontinued status: Because it was a limited edition, the watch is no longer in production, whereas standard Seawolf models were produced for longer periods and are often easier to find on the pre-owned market.

With its distinctive slide rule bezel and chronograph layout, the Navitimer has been the trusted companion of pilots and enthusiasts since 1952.

This model features a 43mm stainless steel case with a bidirectional beaded bezel, glareproof sapphire crystal, and a bold black dial with contrasting silver subdials for optimal legibility. Fitted on a high-quality black leather strap with stainless steel folding clasp, the Navitimer B01 43 combines classic pilot watch aesthetics with modern comfort.

The Breitling Navitimer AB01821A1B1X2 remains one of the most celebrated chronographs in Swiss watchmaking—an enduring symbol of aviation heritage, mechanical mastery, and timeless design.

The Silver Panda Navitimer -- Aviation's Most Iconic Chronograph, Fully Warranted

The Breitling Navitimer is one of the most historically significant pilot's watches ever produced, born in 1952 and adopted as the official timepiece of the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association -- the largest pilots' club in the world -- before famously reaching orbit on the wrist of astronaut Scott Carpenter in 1962. This example is reference AB0138241G1A1, the Silver Panda configuration: silver dial, black sub-dials, 43mm stainless steel case on steel bracelet -- the most sought-after and collectible combination in the current B01 lineup. Power comes from Breitling's in-house COSC-certified Caliber B01, a manufacture movement featuring a column wheel, vertical clutch, 70-hour power reserve, and 47 jewels, visible through the exhibition caseback. The bidirectional slide rule bezel with cambered sapphire crystal remains the functional signature of the Navitimer, unchanged in purpose since 1952 and still the most complete pilot's tool on any wrist today. At $10,300 retail, acquiring this fully warranted, complete-set Silver Panda below new pricing represents real value compression in a configuration that consistently commands premium resale positioning. Factory warranty coverage through 2029 means a buyer is effectively purchasing new-watch protection on a pre-owned price.

This is a rare, complete-set Silver Panda Navitimer B01 with active factory warranty -- the right reference, the right dial, the right price.

This 42mm stainless steel model features a bold black dial with luminous hands and markers, ensuring excellent legibility in any environment. The unidirectional ceramic bezel adds durability and scratch resistance, while the rugged black rubber strap provides a secure and comfortable fit whether you’re in the water or on land.

Powered by the reliable Breitling Caliber 17 automatic movement, this watch offers precision timekeeping and a power reserve of approximately 38 hours. A perfect blend of sporty design and Swiss craftsmanship, the Breitling Superocean is a versatile luxury dive watch that looks just as good with casual attire as it does in more active settings.

Its lightweight 42 mm black ceramic case delivers exceptional durability and a distinct tactical aesthetic that stands out at the range, be it the golf range or gun range. NAMED ONE OF WATCHPRO MAGAZINE’S “HOT 100” - THE TOP 100 INFLUENCERS OF THE U.S. WATCH INDUSTRY. HOW CAN WE SERVE YOU?

1969 BREITLING TOP TIME Vintage "Jim Clark" Panda Dial Chronograph Watch - Stainless Steel

All Original and Never Polished - Beautiful Original Reverse Panda Dial - 1 Year Warranty

Today's offering showcases this fabulously all-original ref. 810 Top Time Mark II chronograph with so-called "reverse panda" dial. Our photos couldn't begin to do it justice!

Its clean look and fantastically well-designed chronograph makes it in many ways the quintessential perfectly balanced chronograph. It has a timeless vintage look that is distinctive yet elegant.

The centerpiece of the watch is its beautiful "reverse panda" dial with black main section and easy to read dial contrasting silver registers. Besides a classic look and "reverse panda" silver registers the dial also features an outer tachometer ring to easily measure the speed of objects, done especially for aviation and auto racing uses. Its orange hand really brings out its racing look and heritage.

Earning the nickname in Breitling's marketing at the time as "Long Playing," this Breitling chronograph has an extra-long 52-hour power reserve and a sub-dial that measures up to 12 elapsed hours.

Built the year man landed it on the moon in 1969, we can't help but feel the space history in this watch with its chronograph and black and silver dial.

It is in as honest condition as they come with all of its original parts and presents very nicely. The case has never been polished.

Because these were "tool" watches they often show much wear and the complicated dials did not refinish well.

Its case presents in excellent no signs of previous polishing and only fairly light wear and one tiny pin prick mark in the back. A light polish could make it present close to mint.

Its untouched original matte black dial retains a strong black color with only light fading over the years. The luminous has darkly toned giving the markers a "ghosted" appearance. It is in excellent condition with only very light aging.

The Breitling Navitimer 1 B01 Chronograph, reference AB0127211B1X1, represents a successful combination of sporty dynamism and classic aircraft interior design. The stainless steel case has a generous diameter of 46 mm and a height of 14.5 mm, creating a striking yet balanced presence on the wrist. The stainless steel bezel rounds off the masculine appearance and complements the black dial design, which offers unmistakable readability with contrasting hands and scales.

The dial is deep black with a feature-rich chronograph layout. Readability is optimized by well-placed subdials and clear indices. The watch is powered by the highly acclaimed B01 automatic caliber, which contains 47 jewels and has a frequency of 28,800 vibrations per hour. The power reserve is a generous approx. 70 hours, which also allows for longer breaks between wear without any problems. One of the core features is the chronograph, which allows for precise start, stop, and reset functions.

The strap is made of high-quality black leather with a stainless steel buckle – a second case that harmonizes perfectly in terms of aesthetics and functionality. The lug width is 24 mm, ensuring that the strap sits securely and comfortably on the wrist. The glass is made of sapphire crystal, which reliably resists scratches. The water resistance is rated at 3 atm, which protects against everyday activities but does not allow diving.

Breitling’s most versatile Navitimer—for the journey. The Navitimer is one of the most recognizable watches ever made. It’s on collectors’ lists of the greatest watches of all time. What began in 1952 as a tool for pilots has gone on to mean something profound to every single person who has had this timepiece along on their personal journey.

The Navitimer Automatic and Automatic GMT 41 distill the iconic look, omitting the chronograph yet preserving a balanced dial composition. The central placement of the 24-hour scale on the GMT and the Automatic’s clean face, ensure the intricate slide rule stands out while streamlining the overall aesthetics. Further refining the collection, the notched bezel was incorporated for a contemporary touch, and alternating polished and brushed finishes add a dynamic play of light. Straps in alligator leather and bracelets with a seamless butterfly clasp offer comfort and convenience. The palette includes colorful dials, encased in a choice of stainless steel or 18 k red gold.

This reinvention of the Navitimer, marrying the classic with the contemporary, is tailored for enthusiasts and the style-conscious alike, promising Breitling’s commitment to heritage and innovation in each timepiece.

The Breitling Navitimer B01 Chronograph 43 Ref. AB01821A1B1X2 is an icon of aviation watchmaking, instantly recognizable for its legendary design and technical excellence. With its distinctive slide rule bezel and chronograph layout, the Navitimer has been the trusted companion of pilots and enthusiasts since 1952.

This model features a 43mm stainless steel case with a bidirectional beaded bezel, glareproof sapphire crystal, and a bold black dial with contrasting silver subdials for optimal legibility. The chronograph counters, date display at 4:30, and red central chronograph hand deliver both precision and functionality, while luminous markers and hands ensure visibility in any light conditions.

At its core is the Breitling Manufacture Caliber B01 automatic movement, offering a 70-hour power reserve, COSC chronometer certification, and a sapphire caseback to view the movement’s finishing.

Fitted on a high-quality black leather strap with stainless steel folding clasp, the Navitimer B01 43 combines classic pilot watch aesthetics with modern comfort.

The Breitling Navitimer AB01821A1B1X2 remains one of the most celebrated chronographs in Swiss watchmaking—an enduring symbol of aviation heritage, mechanical mastery, and timeless design.

The Breitling Callistino (often referred to as Callisto in some contexts) reference D52045 is a sophisticated ladies’ quartz timepiece that blends sporty elegance with luxurious materials. This two-tone model features a polished stainless steel case paired with 18k yellow gold accents on the unidirectional rotating bezel, crown, and bracelet links, creating a timeless and versatile bi-color design.

The approximately 27-30mm case houses a stunning white mother-of-pearl dial, known for its iridescent shimmer that shifts beautifully under light. The dial is adorned with applied 18k yellow gold hour markers (often in Arabic or baton style, with variations including diamond accents in some examples), luminous gold hands for excellent readability, and a discreet date window at the 3 o’clock position.

Protected by a glare-proofed sapphire crystal, the watch offers 100 meters of water resistance, making it suitable for everyday wear. It is powered by a precise Swiss quartz movement and typically comes on a matching two-tone stainless steel and 18k yellow gold Pilot bracelet with a deployant clasp for secure comfort.
This vintage-inspired ladies’ Breitling from around the early 2000s exemplifies the brand’s commitment to robust yet feminine design, perfect for those seeking a blend of luxury and functionality.

Breitling’s most versatile Navitimer—for the journey. The Navitimer is one of the most recognizable watches ever made. It’s on collectors’ lists of the greatest watches of all time. What began in 1952 as a tool for pilots has gone on to mean something profound to every single person who has had this timepiece along on their personal journey. The Navitimer Automatic and Automatic GMT 41 distill the iconic look, omitting the chronograph yet preserving a balanced dial composition. The central placement of the 24-hour scale on the GMT and the Automatic’s clean face, ensure the intricate slide rule stands out while streamlining the overall aesthetics. Further refining the collection, the notched bezel was incorporated for a contemporary touch, and alternating polished and brushed finishes add a dynamic play of light. Straps in alligator leather and bracelets with a seamless butterfly clasp offer comfort and convenience. The palette includes colorful dials, encased in a choice of stainless steel or 18 k red gold. This reinvention of the Navitimer, marrying the classic with the contemporary, is tailored for enthusiasts and the style-conscious alike, promising Breitling’s commitment to heritage and innovation in each timepiece.

For sale is a stunning Breitling Superocean Heritage II in the highly sought-after blue dial configuration. This is a complete, full-set example in excellent condition, representing a rare opportunity to acquire one of Breitling's most iconic sport watches with remaining manufacturer's warranty.
Condition
The watch presents in excellent condition throughout. The case retains its sharp lines with minimal signs of wear, and the sapphire crystal is completely clear with no scratches or marks. The blue ceramic unidirectional bezel is in excellent shape with no chips, fading, or damage. The dial and hands are flawless. The only cosmetic imperfection is a few minor, light scratches on the deployment clasp — consistent with normal everyday wear and not visible during wear. Overall, this watch presents far above average for its age.
The Watch
The Superocean Heritage II is Breitling's love letter to their iconic 1957 dive watch, blending vintage-inspired design with modern Swiss engineering. The sunburst blue dial is one of the most versatile and wearable colorways in the collection — equally at home with a suit as it is with a t-shirt. The blue ceramic bezel adds a cohesive, polished look that has made this reference a favorite among collectors and enthusiasts alike.
Powering the watch is Breitling's Calibre B20 — a robust automatic movement derived from the acclaimed Tudor MT5612 platform, offering an impressive 70-hour power reserve and COSC chronometer certification, ensuring accuracy to within -4/+6 seconds per day.

Performance and style for all your water-based pursuits. In the 1960s, the new sport of scuba diving was all the rage. For divers, timing was everything, and Breitling set out to give them the most clear-cut dive watch possible by eliminating any feature that didn’t support their lifesaving needs underwater. This pared-down watch was named the Slow Motion. What remained were clean lines, chunky luminescent indexes, squared-off hands and a high-contrast dial ring, all aimed at maximum legibility. Practical, yes. But what people really fell in love with was the look. The new Superocean retraces the Slow Motion's celebrated design codes, while adding modern features, like a ceramic-inlayed bezel and brightly colored dials. Broad hands and indexes in luminescent Super-LumiNova allow for easy readability underwater. The watch is water resistant to 300 m (1000 ft), as well as being shock-, sand- and saltwater-resistant. The bronze version uses a special alloy that has excellent resistance to corrosion, yet will still develop a subtle patina over time that makes the watch even more unique. The two strap options (rubber and metal bracelet) have a folding clasp that allows for micro-adjustments of up to 15 mm for easy wear over rashies and wetsuits. Its scratch-proof ceramic inlayed bezel will never wear or fade. For safety, the bezel is uni-directional on most sizes and bi-directional with a patented lock on the 46 mm. Best of all, this is no longer strictly a dive watch—surf with it, swim with it, hit the beach bar with it, then wear it out to dinner. The Superocean perfectly balances the nostalgia of its heritage with a fresh new look.

For Sale is this watch is a Breitling Superocean Professional, reference number A17345. This model is a celebrated "tool watch" from the late 1990s and early 2000s, known for its extreme durability, professional specifications, and bold aesthetic.
Detailed Description
* Dial: is its vibrant Black dial.The dial features:
* Large, luminescent Arabic numerals at 6, 9, and 12 o'clock, with bold circular indices for the other hours.
* An inner 24-hour track for military time.
* The Breitling winged logo and "BREITLING 1884" below the 12 o'clock position.
* The model name "SuperOcean" and specifications "PROFESSIONAL / AUTOMATIC / 5000FT / 1524M" on the lower half.
* A date window at the 3 o'clock position.
* Broad, luminous sword-style hands for the hour and minute, and a distinctive red-tipped seconds hand for easy tracking.
* Case and Bezel: The watch features a robust, satin-brushed stainless steel case, approximately 41.5 mm in diameter.
* The bezel is a unidirectional rotating dive bezel, a critical safety feature for divers to track immersion time. It is also finished in brushed steel and features Breitling's signature "rider tabs" at the 15, 30, and 45-minute marks. These tabs make the bezel easier to grip and turn, even with gloved hands.
* The crown is a large, screw-down type, protected by substantial crown guards, ensuring its water-tightness.
* Water Resistance: One of this model's key selling points is its exceptional water resistance of 5000 feet (1524 meters). This professional-grade specification places it among the elite deep-sea diving watches and was a remarkable engineering feat for its time.
* Movement: This watch is powered by the Breitling Caliber 17, a robust and reliable automatic (self-winding) movement. It is based on the Swiss ETA 2824-2 caliber and is a COSC-certified chronometer, meaning it has passed rigorous tests for accuracy and precision.
* Strap: The watch in the photo is on a Stainless Steel Bracelet.
History and Significance
The Breitling Superocean has a rich history that began long before this model was produced.
* The Original (1957): Breitling launched the first Superocean models in 1957, entering the fiercely competitive market of professional dive watches alongside contemporaries like the Rolex Submariner and Omega Seamaster. These early models were praised for their clean design and superb functionality.
* The 1990s Revival: After Breitling was revitalized under Ernest Schneider in the 1980s, the brand focused heavily on its "Instruments for Professionals" mantra, primarily in aviation. In the mid-1990s, Breitling relaunched the Superocean line with a completely new design philosophy. The goal was to create the ultimate, uncompromising modern dive watch.
* The "Tool Watch" Era (A17345): This yellow-dialed model, the Superocean Professional A17345, is a perfect embodiment of that 1990s ethos. Breitling abandoned the elegant designs of the 50s for a tougher, more industrial, and unapologetically functional aesthetic. The massive water resistance rating was a key part of its identity, showcasing Breitling's technical prowess. This watch was built to be a reliable instrument for the most demanding underwater environments.

From the manufacture:
Breitling’s all-purpose watch for your every pursuit. Introduced in 1984, at a time where extra-thin quartz timepieces were the order of the day, Breitling placed a bold bet on an impressively proportioned mechanical watch. Based on the Frecce Tricolori chronograph, which had been developed and launched in 1983 in collaboration with the famed Italian aerial squadron, the Chronomat celebrated Breitling’s centenary in style and marked the return of the mechanical chronograph to its rightfully prominent place at the brand which had built its global reputation on these incredible watches. The Chronomat is matched to the signature “Rouleaux” bracelet in metal or rubber and is powered by the in-house Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01, a COSC-certified chronometer.

Designed to be both a lightweight watch for athletes and a casual, everyday sports chronograph, the Endurance Pro perfectly blends high precision & innovative technology with a vibrant & colorful design. It is the ultimate athleisure watch. Designed for men and women whose active lives blend a professional mindset with a sporty lifestyle, the Endurance Pro is equal to the challenges of a rigorous workout but fashionable enough for everyday wear. The Endurance Pro features an ultra-light Breitlight case, a robust material 3.3 times lighter than titanium and 5.8 times lighter than stainless steel. Non-magnetic, thermally stable and hypoallergenic, Breitlight is highly resistant to scratches, traction and corrosion. It also has a touch that is warmer than metal and a slightly textured effect accentuating the originality of the design. Exclusive to Breitling, it is 100% Swiss-made. Offered with a set of colorful dial and rubber straps fitting a Breitlight double tang-type buckle, the Endurance Pro can also be paired with one of Breitling’s colorful Outerknown ECONYL yarn single-piece strap. Alright, buckle up—because the Breitling Navitimer A24322 46mm Black Dial with Steel Bracelet isn’t just a watch. It’s a legacy on your wrist, an icon of aviation, and a timepiece that commands attention like few others. This isn’t for the faint-hearted—it’s for the man who values precision, power, and presence.

Why the Navitimer? Because It’s a Watch With a Story.

Since 1952, the Breitling Navitimer has been the definitive pilot’s watch, trusted by aviators and admired by watch enthusiasts worldwide. Worn by astronauts, military pilots, and world travelers, this is more than just a timepiece—it’s a symbol of adventure, ambition, and achievement.

When you wear a Navitimer, you’re not just wearing a watch. You’re wearing history.

Bold. Powerful. Built for the Skies.

The 46mm stainless steel case isn’t just big—it’s bold and unapologetic. It demands attention. The black dial with silver subdials is designed for maximum legibility, just like the cockpit instruments it was inspired by. This is a watch that looks just as good in a business meeting as it does in a pilot’s seat.

And then there’s the bezel—Breitling’s signature slide rule calculator. Before digital computers, pilots used this bezel to calculate fuel consumption, flight times, and airspeed. Today, it’s a mechanical marvel that adds a layer of sophistication and functionality few other watches can match.

Precision That Never Misses a Beat

Inside, the Breitling Caliber 24 automatic movement delivers COSC-certified chronometer accuracy—meaning this watch is more precise than 99% of mechanical watches on the planet.

But that’s not all:

✅ Chronograph (stopwatch function)—because timing is everything.

✅ Dual time zone (GMT function)—perfect for frequent travelers and jet-setter

✅ 42-hour power reserve—so even when you’re off duty, your watch isn’t.

The Bracelet: Steel Strength Meets Refined Elegance

The polished stainless steel bracelet isn’t just about durability—it’s about luxury and presence. It catches the light in a way that makes it impossible to ignore. It’s the kind of bracelet that feels substantial, reassuring you that you’re wearing a serious timepiece.

The Full Set: Because Details Matter

When you invest in this Navitimer, you’re getting:

✔ Original Breitling box and papers—because authenticity is everything.

✔ Warranty card—for peace of mind.

✔ A watch that holds its value—Breitling’s reputation ensures that this is not just a purchase; it’s an investment.

Who Is This Watch For?

The Breitling Navitimer A24322 is for the man who isn’t afraid to stand out. It’s for the leader, the traveler, the risk-taker—the man who knows that time isn’t just something to measure, but something to conquer.

If you want a watch that’s more than just an accessory—if you want a timepiece with heritage, precision, and sheer presence—this is it.

The real question is: Are you ready to own a piece of history?

Like their vintage SuperOcean predecessor, they feature several design elements from the 1957 original such as the concave ceramic bezel, oversized indexes, and arrow-shaped hour hand. One distinctly Scottish detail: a tweed-inspired fabric strap, made of a sumptuous blend of silk and wool. All the watches come on a metal mesh bracelet, while the complementary fabric strap is provided in a separate pouch, along with a Spring Bar tool for easy switches. For those who like to mix and match, an additional blue strap can be purchased separately. Breitling’s most versatile Navitimer—for the journey. The Navitimer is one of the most recognizable watches ever made. It’s on collectors’ lists of the greatest watches of all time. What began in 1952 as a tool for pilots has gone on to mean something profound to every single person who has had this timepiece along on their personal journey. The Navitimer Automatic and Automatic GMT 41 distill the iconic look, omitting the chronograph yet preserving a balanced dial composition. The central placement of the 24-hour scale on the GMT and the Automatic’s clean face, ensure the intricate slide rule stands out while streamlining the overall aesthetics. Further refining the collection, the notched bezel was incorporated for a contemporary touch, and alternating polished and brushed finishes add a dynamic play of light. Straps in alligator leather and bracelets with a seamless butterfly clasp offer comfort and convenience. The palette includes colorful dials, encased in a choice of stainless steel or 18 k red gold. This reinvention of the Navitimer, marrying the classic with the contemporary, is tailored for enthusiasts and the style-conscious alike, promising Breitling’s commitment to heritage and innovation in each timepiece.

We are excited to present this certified pre-owned Breitling Cosmonaute Scott Carpenter timepiece to collectors who appreciate the intersection of history, innovation, and watchmaking. This Breitling is from a limited edition of 1000 pieces for the 35th anniversary of the Project Mercury Space Flights of 1962, originally purchased in August 1997, and is the only known example with Scott Carpenter's signature present in 3 places: the leather calfskin strap; the warranty booklet; and the limited edition certificate. There are also 4 USPS Project Mercury $0.04 postage stamps included. The stainless steel case with slide rule bezel is 43mm in diameter. This Breitling watch features a black dial with silver subdials, and 24-hour display of hours (A feature specifically requested by Scott Carpenter of Breitling for the watch to accompany his pace flight), minutes, small seconds, chronograph with 30 minute and 12 hour counters, and a manual winding movement. The watch comes on a stainless steel bracelet with folding clasp in addition to the Scott Carpenter signed leather strap on a steel folding clasp. This certified pre-owned timepiece come complete with box and papers.

Performance and style for all your water-based pursuits. In the 1960s, the new sport of scuba diving was all the rage. For divers, timing was everything, and Breitling set out to give them the most clear-cut dive watch possible by eliminating any feature that didn’t support their lifesaving needs underwater. This pared-down watch was named the Slow Motion. What remained were clean lines, chunky luminescent indexes, squared-off hands and a high-contrast dial ring, all aimed at maximum legibility. Practical, yes. But what people really fell in love with was the look. The new Superocean retraces the Slow Motion's celebrated design codes, while adding modern features, like a ceramic-inlayed bezel and brightly colored dials. Broad hands and indexes in luminescent Super-LumiNova allow for easy readability underwater. The watch is water resistant to 300 m (1000 ft), as well as being shock-, sand- and saltwater-resistant. The bronze version uses a special alloy that has excellent resistance to corrosion, yet will still develop a subtle patina over time that makes the watch even more unique. The two strap options (rubber and metal bracelet) have a folding clasp that allows for micro-adjustments of up to 15 mm for easy wear over rashies and wetsuits. Its scratch-proof ceramic inlayed bezel will never wear or fade. For safety, the bezel is uni-directional on most sizes and bi-directional with a patented lock on the 46 mm. Best of all, this is no longer strictly a dive watch—surf with it, swim with it, hit the beach bar with it, then wear it out to dinner. The Superocean perfectly balances the nostalgia of its heritage with a fresh new look.

Made to move between the waves and the waterfront.

The Superocean Heritage is no ordinary sea watch - it never was. When Breitling first launched it in 1957, it took a different tack from other dive watches of the era. The Ref. 1004 (a sleek time-only piece) and Ref. 807 (the world's first dedicated dive chronograph) weren't just about exploring the underwater world - they were about looking good doing it. The spear-and-arrow hands, ceramic rotating bezel, and naturally sleek proportions made it a favourite of stylish adventurers who lived for the sea and the scene. Today, that philosophy is still at the core of the Superocean Heritage. It's got the soul of the 1950s, but designed for today.

The latest Superocean Heritage builds on everything that made the original distinctive- and sharpens it. The chronograph is powered by the Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01, Breitling's industry-leading timekeeping movement. A ceramic-inlayed bezel adds exceptional scratch resistance, while integrated mesh-metal or mesh-rubber bracelets fit snugly against the case, secured with folding clasps for a clean, watertight finish. Tone-on-tone subdials, a discreet date window at six o'clock, and the original signature hands- arrow for hours, spear for minutes- ensure legibility and balance, above or below the surface.

Breitling was founded in 1884 by Léon Breitling in Saint-Imier, Switzerland, with a focus on chronographs and precision instruments. Early on, the brand established itself as a specialist in technical watches, producing timepieces for sports, science, and industrial use. In 1915, Breitling introduced one of the world’s first wrist-worn chronographs with a separate pusher—an innovation that would lay the groundwork for the modern chronograph as we know it. By 1934, under Léon’s son Gaston, Breitling refined this with the addition of a second pusher, allowing for independent stop, start, and reset functions—making it a favorite among pilots, engineers, and explorers.

Breitling’s legacy is most closely tied to aviation. In 1952, it introduced the now-iconic Navitimer, a chronograph with a built-in slide rule designed for flight calculations—instantly embraced by pilots and even adopted as the official watch of the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA). Throughout the jet age, Breitling supplied cockpit instruments for aircraft and continued innovating with the launch of models like the Chronomat, Emergency, and Superocean. Today, Breitling combines its rugged, tool-watch heritage with in-house mechanical movements and vintage-inspired designs, cementing its reputation as a brand that blends utility, history, and bold character—on land, in the sea, and above the clouds.”

RARE Breitling Crosswind Chronograph K13055 – Solid 18k Yellow Gold Mens Watch – Full Gold Bracelet – Complete Box & Papers

An exceptional opportunity to own a stunning Breitling Crosswind Chronograph crafted entirely in solid 18k yellow gold, reference K13055. This impressive luxury chronograph combines Breitling’s signature aviation-inspired design with precious-metal craftsmanship, resulting in a truly striking and collectible timepiece.

The Crosswind was one of Breitling’s flagship models of the early 2000s and remains highly sought after today for its bold presence, intricate dial design and robust chronograph functionality. The watch features a beautifully detailed black dial with applied Roman numerals, three chronograph sub-dials and a date window at 3 o’clock, all housed within a substantial solid 18k yellow gold case and matching full 18k gold bracelet.

Powered by the reliable Breitling automatic chronograph movement (COSC certified), this timepiece delivers both precision and timeless luxury.

This particular example comes as a complete collector’s set, making it an especially attractive piece for collectors or enthusiasts seeking a full and well-preserved package.

With gold prices increasing, solid 18k gold watches like this are becoming increasingly desirable, making this a particularly appealing piece for investment, collectors and enthusiasts alike.

Made to move between the waves and the waterfront.

The Superocean Heritage is no ordinary sea watch - it never was. When Breitling first launched it in 1957, it took a different tack from other dive watches of the era. The Ref. 1004 (a sleek time-only piece) and Ref. 807 (the world's first dedicated dive chronograph) weren't just about exploring the underwater world - they were about looking good doing it. The spear-and-arrow hands, ceramic rotating bezel, and naturally sleek proportions made it a favourite of stylish adventurers who lived for the sea and the scene. Today, that philosophy is still at the core of the Superocean Heritage. It's got the soul of the 1950s, but designed for today.

The latest Superocean Heritage builds on everything that made the original distinctive- and sharpens it. The chronograph is powered by the Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01, Breitling's industry-leading timekeeping movement. A ceramic-inlayed bezel adds exceptional scratch resistance, while integrated mesh-metal or mesh-rubber bracelets fit snugly against the case, secured with folding clasps for a clean, watertight finish. Tone-on-tone subdials, a discreet date window at six o'clock, and the original signature hands- arrow for hours, spear for minutes- ensure legibility and balance, above or below the surface.

This watch comes as a Full Set (Box & Papers).

Breitling was founded in 1884 by Léon Breitling in Saint-Imier, Switzerland, with a focus on chronographs and precision instruments. Early on, the brand established itself as a specialist in technical watches, producing timepieces for sports, science, and industrial use. In 1915, Breitling introduced one of the world’s first wrist-worn chronographs with a separate pusher—an innovation that would lay the groundwork for the modern chronograph as we know it. By 1934, under Léon’s son Gaston, Breitling refined this with the addition of a second pusher, allowing for independent stop, start, and reset functions—making it a favorite among pilots, engineers, and explorers.

Breitling’s legacy is most closely tied to aviation. In 1952, it introduced the now-iconic Navitimer, a chronograph with a built-in slide rule designed for flight calculations—instantly embraced by pilots and even adopted as the official watch of the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA). Throughout the jet age, Breitling supplied cockpit instruments for aircraft and continued innovating with the launch of models like the Chronomat, Emergency, and Superocean. Today, Breitling combines its rugged, tool-watch heritage with in-house mechanical movements and vintage-inspired designs, cementing its reputation as a brand that blends utility, history, and bold character—on land, in the sea, and above the clouds.”

The Breitling Top Time B01 Ford Mustang (reference AB01762A1L1X1) is a compelling tribute to the first‑generation Ford Mustang, capturing the spirit of 1960s American motoring with its racing‑green dial and iconic Mustang emblem. Housed in a 41 mm stainless steel case with a thickness of 13.3 mm and a lug‑to‑lug of 50.36 mm, this chronograph wears with presence yet remains well‑balanced on the wrist. Powered by the in‑house Breitling Manufacture Caliber 01 automatic movement, it delivers a robust 70‑hour power reserve and chronograph functions via a column‑wheel, vertical‑clutch architecture. The green dial features contrasting “squircle” subdials and a tachymeter scale, evoking vintage dashboard gauges, while Super‑LumiNova hands and markers ensure legibility. With 100 m water resistance and a perforated brown calfskin racing strap, the watch blends sporty durability with refined vintage styling. Though not a limited edition in the strict sense, its automotive collaboration gives it a niche appeal among collectors seeking character and narrative. The exhibition caseback bearing the Mustang logo adds a layer of mechanical and aesthetic intrigue, making this piece a standout within Breitling’s Top Time line. Like New with Breitling box.

BRAND NEW WITH BOX AND PAPERS
MODEL AB0139A71G1P1
Breitling’s iconic pilot’s chronograph – for the journey. In 1952, Willy Breitling developed a wrist-worn chronograph with a circular slide rule that would allow pilots to perform all necessary flight calculations. Two years later, the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) announced the design as its official timepiece. The “navigation timer” – or Navitimer – was born. The AOPA was (and remains) the largest pilots’ club in the world, counting nearly every US aviator among its ranks. As the defacto pilot’s watch throughout the glory days of civil aviation, the Navitimer was worn by airline captains and aircraft enthusiasts. It even made its way into space on the wrist of astronaut Scott Carpenter in 1962. And it wasn’t only pilots drawn to the watch’s irrepressible aesthetic. Celebrities of the day such as Miles Davis and Serge Gainsbourg were devotees, proving that the Navitimer had style as well as function. There have been many iterations of Breitling’s icon since its debut 70 years ago, but this new Navitimer captures its most classic features, while enhancing them with modern refinements. A flattened slide rule and a domed crystal create the illusion of a sleeker profile. Alternating polished and brushed metal elements give a lustrous, yet understated finish. Most notably, new colors in shades of blue, green and copper, define the updated dial options. And if there is one update sure to spark nostalgia, it’s the return of the AOPA wings to their original position at 12 o’clock. For 70 years, Breitling’s original pilot’s watch has been beloved by aviators and tastemakers in equal measure. Worn by astronauts in space and the biggest stars on earth, it is Breitling’s most iconic timepiece and one of the most recognizable watches of all time.
41mm Brushed & polished stainless steel case.
Stainless steel bi-directional rotating bezel with slide-rule. The circular slide-rule allows pilots to perform necessary flight calculations.

Breitling in house caliber B01 beats at 28,800 vph, contains 47 jewels & has an approximate power reserve of 70 hours.

Built for jet pilots, the Avenger is designed to take on the toughest cockpits and keep coming back for more. Pairing performance with sophisticated modern aviation style, the collection’s materials—stainless steel on regular models; ceramic and titanium on Night Mission —are the stuff of aviation design, while dial colors are drawn from military uniform shades. All stainless-steel versions come with a choice of military leather strap with a folding-pin buckle or three-row stainless-steel bracelet with micro-adjustable folding clasp. Ceramic models are paired to a military leather strap with a titanium folding-pin buckle. And whether you can pull off a barrel roll or not, you’ll appreciate everything about the Avenger’s 300 m water resistance, easy-grip functionality, and corrosion-resistant construction.

Eine Legende am Handgelenk: Die Breitling Navitimer vereint seit 1952 fliegerische Präzision mit zeitlosem Design. Diese Herrenuhr aus der aktuellen Kollektion trägt die Seele der Aviation in sich – mit ihrer charakteristischen Rechenschieber-Lünette, die einst Piloten durch den Himmel navigierte, und einem silbernen Zifferblatt im edlen Sonnenschliff, das von kontrastierenden schwarzen Hilfszifferblättern akzentuiert wird.
Im Herzen schlägt das Breitling Manufakturkaliber B01, ein COSC-zertifizierter Chronometer mit beeindruckenden 70 Stunden Gangreserve. Das 43 Millimeter Edelstahlgehäuse mit seiner Mischung aus polierten und gebürsteten Oberflächen fängt das Licht auf subtile Weise ein, während das schwarze Lederarmband mit Faltschließe höchsten Tragekomfort verspricht.
Diese Navitimer präsentiert sich in sehr gutem Gesamtzustand und wird im Full Set komplett mit der originalen Breitling Uhrenbox im Reiseetui-Stil, Garantiekarte sowie der Erstkaufrechnung des renommierten Konzessionärs Wempe aus dem Jahr 2022 geliefert. Die Herstellergarantie läuft noch bis Oktober 2027 – für unbeschwerten Genuss dieser faszinierenden Zeitmesser-Ikone.

- Navitimer B01 aus der aktuellen 70-Jahre-Jubiläumskollektion (2022)

100% AUTHENTIC BRAND NEW BREITLING NAVITIMER B01 CHRONOGRAPH 41 SILVER DIAL BLUE ALLIGATOR STRAP WATCH.
BRAND NEW WITH BOX AND PAPERS
MODEL AB0139A71G1P1
Breitling’s iconic pilot’s chronograph – for the journey. In 1952, Willy Breitling developed a wrist-worn chronograph with a circular slide rule that would allow pilots to perform all necessary flight calculations. Two years later, the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) announced the design as its official timepiece. The “navigation timer” – or Navitimer – was born. The AOPA was (and remains) the largest pilots’ club in the world, counting nearly every US aviator among its ranks. As the defacto pilot’s watch throughout the glory days of civil aviation, the Navitimer was worn by airline captains and aircraft enthusiasts. It even made its way into space on the wrist of astronaut Scott Carpenter in 1962. And it wasn’t only pilots drawn to the watch’s irrepressible aesthetic. Celebrities of the day such as Miles Davis and Serge Gainsbourg were devotees, proving that the Navitimer had style as well as function. There have been many iterations of Breitling’s icon since its debut 70 years ago, but this new Navitimer captures its most classic features, while enhancing them with modern refinements. A flattened slide rule and a domed crystal create the illusion of a sleeker profile. Alternating polished and brushed metal elements give a lustrous, yet understated finish. Most notably, new colors in shades of blue, green and copper, define the updated dial options. And if there is one update sure to spark nostalgia, it’s the return of the AOPA wings to their original position at 12 o’clock. For 70 years, Breitling’s original pilot’s watch has been beloved by aviators and tastemakers in equal measure. Worn by astronauts in space and the biggest stars on earth, it is Breitling’s most iconic timepiece and one of the most recognizable watches of all time.
41mm Brushed & polished stainless steel case.
Stainless steel bi-directional rotating bezel with slide-rule. The circular slide-rule allows pilots to perform necessary flight calculations.
Silver dial with Blue Hands.
Features the AOPA wings logo at 12 o'clock.
Blue hour & minute hands with luminous fill.
Blue index hour markers with luminous tips.
Fine printed minutes/seconds & 1/4 seconds fractions track.
Small seconds sub-dial at 9 o'clock.
Date window at 6 o'clock.
Chronograph/Stopwatch functions:
Column wheel mechanism.
Large Blue central seconds hand counter.
30 minute counter sub-dial at 3 o'clock.
12 hour counter sub-dial at 6 o'clock.
Breitling in house caliber B01 beats at 28,800 vph, contains 47 jewels & has an approximate power reserve of 70 hours.

The Chronomat Automatic GMT 40 is Breitling’s tribute to travel, not only for its dual time zone display, but also because of its easygoing aesthetics. Subtle dial colors, combined with an all-steel case and bracelet, work with any wardrobe. Meanwhile, the tone-on-tone 24-hour scale lets the red GMT hand speak for itself. The Breitling Caliber 32 powers the GMT complication. Its 24-hour scale lets the user track a second time zone and know at a glance whether it’s day or night, while the “onion” crown (a classic Chronomat feature named for its fluted-dome shape) allows for easy adjustments. As an all-purpose sports watch, meant to cross over between Breitling’s air, land and sea universes, the GMT 40 has an impressive 200m water resistance. Known as “a watch for every pursuit,” the Chronomat mission is truer now than ever because of the GMT 40’s practical size and understated style. Sturdy enough for the gym, yet smart enough for black tie: If there’s one watch to travel with, this is it.

For sale is one of my absolute favorites in the Breitling catalog, the Superocean Heritage 42, reference AB2010, a modern dive watch that blends classic styling with a strong, contemporary dial.

The standout feature here is the green sunray dial. In softer light it appears deep and almost understated, but when the light hits it directly the dial comes alive with a vibrant sunburst effect. The tone shifts throughout the day, giving the watch a lot more personality than a typical monochrome diver.

The design of the Superocean Heritage line pulls directly from Breitling’s original 1957 Superocean, with the distinctive arrow hour hand, minimalist markers, and clean bezel layout. It has a vintage influence, but the finishing and proportions keep it feeling firmly modern.

The 42mm stainless steel case wears comfortably on the wrist and strikes a good balance between presence and everyday wearability. The polished ceramic bezel adds depth and durability while maintaining the sleek profile that defines the Heritage series.

Inside is the Breitling Caliber B20, an automatic movement based on the Tudor MT5612 architecture. It’s COSC-certified, offers a 70-hour power reserve, and is known for its durability and accuracy.
The result is a diver that feels refined enough for daily wear while still carrying the DNA of a true tool watch.

Executive Summary:

The Breitling Superocean Heritage 46 (Ref. A17320) blends vintage dive-watch design with modern Swiss engineering. Inspired by Breitling’s original 1957 Superocean, this bold 46mm diver offers unmistakable wrist presence, exceptional build quality, and a clean heritage aesthetic that has made the Superocean Heritage line a favorite among collectors and enthusiasts alike.

This example is offered in good used condition and comes as a complete set with original boxes, warranty certificate, paperwork, Breitling buckle, and a new uncut rubber strap, making it an excellent opportunity to acquire a well-regarded Breitling diver with strong everyday wearability.

Brand Heritage:

Founded in 1884 by Léon Breitling, Breitling has built a reputation as one of Switzerland’s most respected manufacturers of professional-grade watches. The brand became globally recognized for its precision chronographs and strong ties to aviation, exploration, and professional tool watches.

While Breitling is often associated with aviation, the company has also produced some of the most capable and recognizable dive watches in Swiss watchmaking. The Superocean line, introduced in 1957, was designed specifically for underwater exploration and professional diving.

Today, Breitling remains synonymous with robust engineering, exceptional legibility, and purposeful design, making its watches highly respected in the pre-owned luxury watch market.

About the Model:

The Superocean Heritage collection pays tribute to Breitling’s original 1957 dive watch while incorporating modern materials and movement technology.

The 46mm Superocean Heritage is the boldest interpretation of this vintage-inspired design. Its large case size, clean dial layout, and polished steel case create a distinctive balance between classic dive watch styling and modern luxury sports watch presence.

Unlike many contemporary dive watches, the Superocean Heritage retains elegant vintage cues such as the minimalist dial layout and refined case finishing, making it equally appropriate for casual wear, travel, or professional settings.

About the Movement:
The watch is powered by the Breitling Caliber 17, a Swiss automatic movement based on the highly respected ETA 2824-2 architecture, which Breitling modifies and regulates to meet its stringent standards.

Key characteristics include:
• Automatic self-winding mechanical movement
• COSC-certified chronometer accuracy (in many production examples)
• Approximately 38–42 hour power reserve
• Quick-set date function
• High reliability and global serviceability

The movement is widely regarded for its durability, accuracy, and ease of maintenance, making it an ideal choice for a daily-wear luxury sports watch.

Built for jet pilots, the Avenger is designed to take on the toughest cockpits and keep coming back for more. But you don’t need air force credentials to appreciate its bold design, exceptional resilience, and next-level functionality. Pairing performance with sophisticated modern aviation style, the collection’s materials—stainless steel on regular models; ceramic and titanium on Night Mission —are the stuff of aviation design, while dial colors are drawn from military uniform shades. All stainless-steel versions come with a choice of military leather strap with a folding-pin buckle or three-row stainless-steel bracelet with micro-adjustable folding clasp. Ceramic models are paired to a military leather strap with a titanium folding-pin buckle. And whether you can pull off a barrel roll or not, you’ll appreciate everything about the Avenger’s 300 m water resistance, easy-grip functionality, and corrosion-resistant construction.

The Breitling Avenger Automatic 42 is a robust and versatile timepiece, designed for adventurers who value precision and reliability. Featuring a striking blue dial with luminescent hands and markers, housed in a polished stainless steel case, this watch offers excellent readability in all lighting conditions. Powered by the Breitling Caliber 17, a COSC-certified self-winding mechanical movement, it offers a power reserve of approximately 38 hours. The unidirectional ratcheted bezel, screw-locked crown, and glare-proof sapphire crystal enhance both functionality and durability. Paired with a stainless steel bracelet, this watch combines style and performance. The watch is unworn, in like-new condition, and comes complete with its original box, papers, and warranty documents dated 2025.
